title: Contributing to the gsm Universe
text: Thank interest contributing gsm suite packages! repository follows centralized gsm Contributor Guidelines maintained gsm.core. Please review guidelines opening issues submitting pull requests.

title: Contributing to the gsm Universe
text: File issue opening pull request. Use appropriate issue template. Track issue gsm Roadmap Project Board. code changes made branch submitted PR following guidelines linked . detailed workflows (branching, releases, QC, CI/CD), please see full Contributor Guidelines gsm.core.

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: Domain Registry centralized system gsm.datasim defines clinical domains (datasets) generated managed. provides structured approach data generation extensible, maintainable, consistent across different study types. vignette explains domain registry works, use existing domains, extend new domains.

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: clinical trial terminology, “domain” refers specific type clinical data, : AE: Adverse Events LB: Laboratory Data VISIT: Visit Records PD: Pharmacodynamics PK: Pharmacokinetics QUERY: Data Queries domain data structure, generation rules, dependencies.

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: domain registry named list entry defines: Dataset name: raw dataset identifier (e.g., “Raw_AE”) Package: R package contains generation function Workflow path: find mapping specifications Generation function: create data Dependencies: domains domain needs Count functions: many records generate Argument builders: prepare function arguments

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
code: # Get the complete domain registry registry <- get_domain_registry()  # View available domains names(registry)  # Examine a specific domain entry str(registry$Raw_AE)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: Adverse Events critical domain clinical trials RBQM, specifically:
code: # View AE domain configuration ae_config <- registry$Raw_AE  # Key components: # - dataset: "Raw_AE" # - package: "gsm.mapping"  # - required_inputs: data, previous_data, combined_specs, etc. # - count_fn: Uses ae_count from snapshot configuration # - generate_fn: Calls Raw_AE() function  print(ae_config$dataset) print(ae_config$package) print(ae_config$required_inputs)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: Laboratory data represents test results time:
code: # View LB domain configuration   lb_config <- registry$Raw_LB  # Key characteristics: # - Uses subject_count for record generation # - Includes visit-based data splitting # - Supports longitudinal data patterns  print(lb_config$count_fn) print(lb_config$args_builder)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
code: # Get all available domains from the registry available_domains <- get_available_domains() print(available_domains)  # Get domains for a specific package mapping_domains <- get_available_domains(package = "gsm.mapping") print(mapping_domains)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: registry system used automatically configure studies:
code: # Create a study configuration config <- create_study_config(   study_id = "REGISTRY001",   participant_count = 100,   site_count = 5 )  # Add domains - these will use registry definitions config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_AE", enabled = TRUE) config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_LB", enabled = TRUE)  # Generate data using registry raw_data <- generate_study_data(config, verbose = TRUE)  # The registry handles: # - Loading appropriate generation functions # - Managing dependencies between domains   # - Applying correct count formulas # - Handling multi-package scenarios

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: domains depend others. registry manages automatically:
code: # Check domain dependencies check_domain_dependencies <- function(domain_name) {   registry <- get_domain_registry()   domain_config <- registry[[domain_name]]      if (!is.null(domain_config$dependencies)) {     return(domain_config$dependencies)   } else {     return("No explicit dependencies defined")   } }  # Check dependencies for various domains check_domain_dependencies("Raw_AE") check_domain_dependencies("Raw_LB")  # Core domains (STUDY, SITE, SUBJ, ENROLL) are always required core_domains <- c("Raw_STUDY", "Raw_SITE", "Raw_SUBJ", "Raw_ENROLL") print("Core required domains:") print(core_domains)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: registry supports domains different packages:
code: # Create a domain-package mapping domain_package_df <- data.frame(   domain = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT", "CustomDomain"),   package = c("gsm.mapping", "gsm.mapping", "gsm.mapping", "custom.package"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  # Create study with multi-package support config <- create_study_config(   study_id = "MULTI001",   participant_count = 50,   site_count = 3 )  config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_AE", enabled = TRUE) config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_LB", enabled = TRUE)  # Generate with package specification raw_data <- generate_raw_data_for_endpoints(config, domain_package_df)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: Different domains different temporal patterns:
code: # Get timeline information for a domain ae_timeline <- get_domain_timeline("Raw_AE") print(ae_timeline)  lb_timeline <- get_domain_timeline("Raw_LB")  print(lb_timeline)  # Timeline shows: # - When domain data typically starts appearing # - How frequently it's collected # - Growth patterns over time # - Dependencies on other domains

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: registry handles different growth patterns:
code: # Domains have different growth patterns:  # Linear growth (steady increase) config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_AE", enabled = TRUE,                             growth_pattern = "linear")  # Exponential growth (accelerating increase)   config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_QUERY", enabled = TRUE,                            growth_pattern = "exponential")  # Constant (same amount each snapshot) config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_VISIT", enabled = TRUE,                             growth_pattern = "constant")  # Custom formulas config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_LB", enabled = TRUE,                            count_formula = function(config) {                              config$study_params$participant_count * 3  # 3 per participant                            })

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: domain specific generation function (e.g., Raw_AE(), Raw_LB()):
code: # Domain generation functions typically take these parameters: # - data: Current snapshot data # - previous_data: Previous snapshot(s) for continuity   # - combined_specs: Data generation specifications # - n: Number of records to generate # - startDate/endDate: Time boundaries # - split_vars: How to distribute data across timepoints  # Example: AE generation function signature # Raw_AE(data, previous_data, combined_specs, n, startDate, endDate, split_vars)  # Example: LB generation function signature   # Raw_LB(data, previous_data, combined_specs, n, startDate, split_vars)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: can customize record counts determined:
code: # Create a study with custom count logic config <- create_study_config(   study_id = "CUSTOM-COUNTS",   participant_count = 100,   site_count = 10 )  # Custom AE count: more AEs at larger sites config <- add_dataset_config(   config,    "Raw_AE",    enabled = TRUE,   count_formula = function(config) {     # Complex formula based on site size distribution     base_ae_rate <- 0.8  # 80% of participants have AEs     site_size_factor <- config$study_params$participant_count / config$study_params$site_count     round(config$study_params$participant_count * base_ae_rate * (site_size_factor / 10))   } )  # Custom LB count: weekly labs for first month, then monthly config <- add_dataset_config(   config,   "Raw_LB",   enabled = TRUE,   count_formula = function(config) {     participants <- config$study_params$participant_count     # Assuming monthly snapshots     participants * 4  # 4 lab visits per participant per month   } )

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: can extend registry custom domains:
code: # Define a custom domain add_custom_domain_to_registry <- function() {   # Get current registry   current_registry <- get_domain_registry()      # Add custom domain   current_registry$Raw_CUSTOM <- list(     dataset = "Raw_CUSTOM",     package = "gsm.mapping",  # or your custom package     workflow_path = "workflow/1_mappings",     required_inputs = c("data", "combined_specs", "n", "start_date"),     count_fn = function(counts, snapshot_idx) {       # Custom count logic       counts$participant_count[snapshot_idx] * 2  # 2 records per participant     },     args_builder = function(context) {       list(         context$data,         context$combined_specs,          n = context$n,         startDate = context$start_date,         custom_param = "custom_value"       )     },     generate_fn = function(args) {       # This would call your custom generation function       # do.call(Raw_CUSTOM, args)       warning("Raw_CUSTOM function not implemented")       return(data.frame())     }   )      return(current_registry) }  # Example usage (this would require implementing Raw_CUSTOM function) # extended_registry <- add_custom_domain_to_registry()

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: create new domains, ’ll need generation functions:
code: # Example structure for a custom domain generation function Raw_CUSTOM <- function(data, combined_specs, n, startDate, custom_param = NULL) {   # Your custom data generation logic here      # Basic structure:   custom_data <- data.frame(     studyid = rep(data$Raw_STUDY$studyid[1], n),     usubjid = sample(data$Raw_SUBJ$usubjid, n, replace = TRUE),     custom_field = paste("CUSTOM", 1:n, sep = "_"),     custom_date = seq(as.Date(startDate),                        as.Date(startDate) + n - 1,                        by = "day")[1:n],     custom_value = runif(n, 0, 100),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   )      return(custom_data) }  # To use this in the registry: # 1. Define the function in your package # 2. Add registry entry as shown above   # 3. Include the domain in your study configuration

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: registry supports migration different data generation approaches:
code: # The package includes migration utilities for moving from # older approaches to the registry-based system  # Check which domains can be migrated available_for_migration <- get_available_domains() print("Domains available for registry-based generation:") print(available_for_migration)  # Migration typically involves: # 1. Updating study configurations to use registry # 2. Converting custom domain logic to registry format   # 3. Testing compatibility with existing workflows

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
code: # Validate registry configuration validate_domain_registry <- function(registry = NULL) {   if (is.null(registry)) {     registry <- get_domain_registry()   }      validation_results <- list()      for (domain_name in names(registry)) {     domain_config <- registry[[domain_name]]          # Check required fields     required_fields <- c("dataset", "package", "generate_fn")     missing_fields <- setdiff(required_fields, names(domain_config))          # Check function availability     package_available <- requireNamespace(domain_config$package, quietly = TRUE)          validation_results[[domain_name]] <- list(       missing_fields = missing_fields,       package_available = package_available,       has_count_fn = !is.null(domain_config$count_fn),       has_args_builder = !is.null(domain_config$args_builder)     )   }      return(validation_results) }  # Validate current registry validation <- validate_domain_registry() str(validation, max.level = 2)

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: Use existing domains possible: registry includes well-tested domains common clinical data types. Follow naming conventions: Use “Raw_” prefix dataset names maintain consistency. Define clear dependencies: domain needs data domains, specify dependencies clearly. Test thoroughly: Validate custom domains across different study configurations. Document extensions: Provide clear documentation custom domains add.

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
code: # Registry-based generation is optimized for performance:  # 1. Efficient package loading - only loads needed packages # 2. Dependency resolution - generates domains in correct order # 3. Multi-package support - handles complex study configurations # 4. Caching - reuses specifications across snapshots  # For large studies, consider: # - Enabling verbose mode to monitor progress # - Breaking large studies into smaller chunks # - Using appropriate snapshot intervals

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
code: # Common issues and solutions:  # Issue: Domain not found in registry # Solution: Check available domains and spelling available <- get_available_domains() print(available)  # Issue: Package not available # Solution: Install required packages missing_packages <- c() registry <- get_domain_registry() for (domain in names(registry)) {   pkg <- registry[[domain]]$package   if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {     missing_packages <- c(missing_packages, pkg)   } } if (length(missing_packages) > 0) {   cat("Missing packages:", paste(missing_packages, collapse = ", "), "\n") }  # Issue: Generation function errors   # Solution: Check function parameters and data availability # Use verbose mode to see detailed error messages

title: Understanding the Domain Registry
text: Domain Registry provides robust, extensible framework clinical data generation gsm.datasim. Key benefits include: Standardization: Consistent approach domain definition generation Extensibility: Easy add new domains customize existing ones Maintainability: Centralized configuration reduces duplication Multi-package support: Domains can come different R packages Dependency management: Automatic handling domain dependencies Understanding registry system enables : - Use existing domains effectively - Create custom domains specialized needs - Maintain complex multi-domain studies - Migrate older generation approaches registry system designed grow needs maintaining backward compatibility ease use.

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: gsm.datasim package provides comprehensive framework generating synthetic clinical trial data. vignette demonstrates set single snapshot longitudinal studies, covering key functions configuration options available.

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: package supports two main types studies: Single Snapshot Studies: Generate data single time point Longitudinal Studies: Generate data across multiple time points simulate study progression

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: single snapshot study generates clinical data one specific time point. useful testing analytics workflows need static dataset.

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: simplest way create single snapshot study using study configuration system:
code: # Create a basic study configuration config <- create_study_config(   study_id = "STUDY001",   participant_count = 100,   site_count = 5 )  # Add clinical domains (datasets) config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_AE", enabled = TRUE)     # Adverse Events config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_LB", enabled = TRUE)     # Laboratory Data config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_VISIT", enabled = TRUE)  # Visit Data  # Generate the study data raw_data <- generate_study_data(config, verbose = TRUE)  # Examine the generated data structure names(raw_data)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: Even single snapshots, can control temporal aspects:
code: # Create configuration with specific temporal settings config <- create_study_config(   study_id = "STUDY002",    participant_count = 200,   site_count = 10 )  # Set temporal configuration config <- set_temporal_config(   config,   start_date = "2023-06-01",   snapshot_count = 1,           # Single snapshot   snapshot_width = "months" )  # Add domains with custom configurations config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_AE", enabled = TRUE) config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_LB", enabled = TRUE,                              growth_pattern = "exponential")  # Generate data raw_data <- generate_study_data(config, verbose = TRUE)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: can run analytics single snapshot study. Results returned -gsm.core pipeline — named list snapshot contains $results (named workflow, e.g. Analysis_kri0001), $mapped, $lWorkflow, $summary:
code: # Create study with analytics config <- create_study_config(   study_id = "STUDY003",   participant_count = 150,   site_count = 8,   analytics_package = "gsm.kri" )  # Add clinical domains config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_AE", enabled = TRUE) config <- add_dataset_config(config, "Raw_LB", enabled = TRUE)  # Generate data raw_data <- generate_study_data(config, verbose = TRUE)  # Run analytics pipeline — returns raw gsm.core results per snapshot analytics_results <- generate_analytics_layers(raw_data, config, verbose = TRUE)  # Each snapshot contains $results, $mapped, $lWorkflow, $summary names(analytics_results[[1]])  # Access a specific metric result across all its data frames kri_result <- analytics_results[[1]]$results$Analysis_kri0001 names(kri_result)  # e.g. Analysis_Summary, Analysis_Flagged, Analysis_Analyzed

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: reporting pipeline runs gsm.reporting workflows top analytics results. Pass analytics output directly generate_reporting_layers():
code: # Run reporting pipeline on analytics results reporting_results <- generate_reporting_layers(   analytics_results = analytics_results,   config = config,   verbose = TRUE )  # Results are keyed by snapshot name, matching analytics_results names(reporting_results)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: Longitudinal studies simulate data evolution time, essential understanding clinical metrics change throughout study.

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: create_longitudinal_study() function provides easiest way set longitudinal study. default runs analytics pipeline; set run_reporting = TRUE also run reporting pipeline:
code: # Create a longitudinal study with default settings study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "LONG-001",   participants = 100,   sites = 5,   snapshots = 6,                    # 6 time points   interval = "1 month",             # Monthly snapshots   domains = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT"), # Clinical domains to include   run_analytics = TRUE,             # Run analytics pipeline   run_reporting = TRUE,             # Also run reporting pipeline   verbose = TRUE )  # Examine the study structure names(study)  # includes $raw_data, $analytics, $reporting  # View snapshot dates names(study$raw_data)  # Analytics results: raw gsm.core output per snapshot if (!is.null(study$analytics)) {   names(study$analytics[[1]]$results)  # e.g. "Analysis_kri0001", ... }  # Reporting results per snapshot if (!is.null(study$reporting)) {   names(study$reporting) }

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: control longitudinal study setup:
code: # Create a complex longitudinal study study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "ADVANCED-001",   participants = 500,   sites = 25,   snapshots = 12,                               # 1 year of monthly data   interval = "1 month",   domains = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT", "PD", "PK"), # Multiple domains   run_analytics = TRUE,   analytics_package = "gsm.kri",           # Specify analytics package   verbose = TRUE )  # Generate study summary summary_data <- summarize_longitudinal_study(study) print(summary_data)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: can specify custom starting date longitudinal study:
code: # Use the lower-level function for more control mappings <- ensure_core_mappings(c("AE", "LB", "VISIT"))  raw_data <- generate_study_snapshots(   study_id = "CUSTOM-001",   participants = 100,   sites = 8,   snapshots = 6,   interval = "2 months",                        # Bi-monthly snapshots   mappings = mappings,   base_date = "2022-01-15",                    # Custom start date   verbose = TRUE )  # Create study object config <- list(   participants = 100,   sites = 8,   snapshots = 6,   interval = "2 months",   domains = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT") )  study <- create_longitudinal_study_data(   study_id = "CUSTOM-001",   raw_data = raw_data,   config = config )

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: package supports various time intervals longitudinal studies:
code: # Weekly snapshots weekly_study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "WEEKLY-001",   participants = 50,   sites = 3,   snapshots = 12,   interval = "1 week",              # Weekly intervals   domains = c("AE", "VISIT"),   verbose = TRUE )  # Quarterly snapshots   quarterly_study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "QUARTERLY-001",   participants = 300,   sites = 15,   snapshots = 8,   interval = "3 months",            # Quarterly intervals     domains = c("AE", "LB", "PD"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: rapid prototyping, use quick_longitudinal_study() function. analytics reporting run default include_pipeline = TRUE:
code: # Create a study with sensible defaults — analytics and reporting both run by default quick_study <- quick_longitudinal_study(   study_name = "QUICK-PROTO-001",   participants = 200,   sites = 10,   months_duration = 18,             # 18-month study   study_type = "standard",   include_pipeline = TRUE,          # default: runs analytics + reporting   verbose = TRUE )  # Analytics and reporting results are available on the study object names(quick_study)  # includes $analytics and $reporting

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: Choose clinical domains based study objectives:
code: # Oncology study domains oncology_domains <- c("AE", "LB", "PD", "OverallResponse",                       "VISIT", "DATACHG", "QUERY")  # Safety study domains   safety_domains <- c("AE", "LB", "VISIT", "EXCLUSION",                    "Death", "STUDCOMP")  # PK/PD study domains pkpd_domains <- c("PK", "PD", "LB", "VISIT", "AE")  # Create study with oncology focus onc_study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "ONCOLOGY-001",   participants = 300,   sites = 20,   snapshots = 24,   interval = "1 month",    domains = oncology_domains,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: Configure analytics package specific workflows, optionally customise reporting package. analytics pipeline returns raw gsm.core results per snapshot; pass directly reporting pipeline. can also run pipelines separately existing study object:
code: # Create study with specific analytics workflows study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "MONITORED-001",   participants = 400,   sites = 30,   snapshots = 12,   interval = "1 month",   domains = c("AE", "LB", "VISIT", "QUERY"),   run_analytics = TRUE,   analytics_package = "gsm.kri",   analytics_workflows = c("kri0001", "kri0002"), # Specific workflows   run_reporting = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )  # Access analytics: $results keyed by workflow name if (!is.null(study$analytics)) {   first_snap <- study$analytics[[1]]    # All metric results for the first snapshot   metric_names <- names(first_snap$results)    # Access a specific metric and its data frames   kri <- first_snap$results[[metric_names[1]]]   kri$Analysis_Summary   # summary-level data frame   kri$Analysis_Flagged   # flagged entities   kri$Analysis_Analyzed  # full analyzed data }  # Access reporting: keyed by snapshot date if (!is.null(study$reporting)) {   names(study$reporting)        # snapshot dates   study$reporting[[1]]          # reporting output for first snapshot } # Build raw data only study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "STEPWISE-001",   participants = 200,   sites = 10,   snapshots = 6,   interval = "1 month",   domains = c("AE", "LB"),   run_analytics = FALSE   # skip pipelines for now )  # Run analytics when ready study <- run_longitudinal_analytics(study, verbose = TRUE)  # Run reporting on top of analytics study <- run_longitudinal_reporting(study, verbose = TRUE)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
code:
# After generating a study, examine the data structure study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id = "EXPLORE-001",   participants = 50,   sites = 3,   snapshots = 3,   interval = "1 month",   domains = c("AE", "LB"),   verbose = TRUE,   run_analytics = FALSE )  # Check data for each snapshot for (i in 1:length(study$raw_data)) {   snapshot_name <- names(study$raw_data)[i]   snapshot_data <- study$raw_data[[i]]      cat("Snapshot:", snapshot_name, "
")   cat("Datasets:", paste(names(snapshot_data), collapse = ", "), "
")      # Check sample sizes   if ("Raw_SUBJ" %in% names(snapshot_data)) {     cat("Subjects:", nrow(snapshot_data$Raw_SUBJ), "
")   }   if ("Raw_AE" %in% names(snapshot_data)) {     cat("AE Records:", nrow(snapshot_data$Raw_AE), "
")    }   cat("
") }

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
code: # Function to validate study data validate_study_data <- function(study) {   validation_results <- list()      for (snapshot_name in names(study$raw_data)) {     snapshot <- study$raw_data[[snapshot_name]]          # Check required datasets     required_datasets <- c("Raw_STUDY", "Raw_SITE", "Raw_SUBJ", "Raw_ENROLL")     missing_required <- setdiff(required_datasets, names(snapshot))          validation_results[[snapshot_name]] <- list(       datasets_present = names(snapshot),       missing_required = missing_required,       total_subjects = if ("Raw_SUBJ" %in% names(snapshot)) nrow(snapshot$Raw_SUBJ) else 0,       total_sites = if ("Raw_SITE" %in% names(snapshot)) nrow(snapshot$Raw_SITE) else 0     )   }      return(validation_results) }  # Validate a study validation <- validate_study_data(study) str(validation)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: complete study object — raw data, analytics, (optionally) reporting — can write everything disk export_study_data(). function creates structured folder hierarchy root directory: Folders created corresponding data exists snapshot.
code: <output_dir>/<study_id>/   <snapshot_date>/     raw/          # Raw_*.csv     mapped/       # Mapped_*.csv  (present when analytics ran)     analytics/    # <metric>_<table>.csv  (present when analytics ran)     reporting/    # Reporting_*.csv  (present when reporting ran)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
code: # Generate a small study to export study <- create_longitudinal_study(   study_id     = "EXPORT-001",   participants = 100,   sites        = 5,   snapshots    = 3,   interval     = "1 month",   domains      = c("AE", "LB"),   run_analytics = TRUE,   run_reporting = TRUE,   verbose       = FALSE )  # Export to a temporary directory — returns the path to the study folder invisibly study_path <- export_study_data(   study      = study,   output_dir = tempdir(),   verbose    = TRUE       # prints per-snapshot progress )  # Confirm the folder structure list.dirs(study_path, full.names = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
code: # Write to a specific project folder study_path <- export_study_data(   study        = study,   output_dir   = "~/my_studies",    # root folder   study_folder = "EXPORT-001-v2",   # override the auto-generated folder name   overwrite    = TRUE               # allow writing into an existing folder )

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: analytics pipeline stores workflow lists, summaries, objects flattened CSV. Set save_rds = TRUE write companion analytics_full.rds file per snapshot:
code: study_path <- export_study_data(   study      = study,   output_dir = tempdir(),   overwrite  = TRUE,   save_rds   = TRUE   # also writes analytics_full.rds per snapshot )  # Reload the full analytics object for a specific snapshot analytics_snap <- readRDS(file.path(study_path, names(study$raw_data)[1],                                     "analytics_full.rds")) names(analytics_snap)  # $results, $mapped, $lWorkflow, $summary

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
code: # List all CSV files written across all snapshots all_csvs <- list.files(study_path, pattern = "\\.csv$", recursive = TRUE) cat(length(all_csvs), "CSV files written\n")  # Raw data lives in <snapshot>/raw/ raw_csvs <- all_csvs[grepl("/raw/", all_csvs)] cat("Raw CSVs:", paste(basename(raw_csvs), collapse = ", "), "\n")  # Read a specific snapshot's adverse event data back in snap_date <- names(study$raw_data)[1] ae_path   <- file.path(study_path, snap_date, "raw", "Raw_AE.csv") ae_df     <- read.csv(ae_path) nrow(ae_df)

title: Setting up Clinical Studies with gsm.datasim
text: gsm.datasim package provides flexible tools creating single snapshot longitudinal clinical studies. Key takeaways: Use create_study_config() generate_study_data() single snapshots Use create_longitudinal_study() multi-snapshot studies Configure domains based study type monitoring needs Leverage analytics pipeline automated quality monitoring Use quick_longitudinal_study() rapid prototyping Use export_study_data() write completed studies structured folder hierarchy modular design allows start simple add complexity needed, making suitable exploratory work production data generation scenarios.

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {gsm.datasim: Synthetic Test Data Generator},   author = {Roman Rogoza and Laura Maxwell and Zelos Zhu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.1.2},   url = {https://github.com/Gilead-BioStats/gsm.datasim}, }

title: Synthetic Test Data Generator
text: gsm.datasim package provides functionality produce sample data use testing development applications packages related Risk-Based Quality Monitoring (RBQM) Clinical Trials. Given inputs regarding synthetic Study’s desired ID, Participant Count, Site Count, Snapshot Count, Key Risk Indicators constructed, package generates data pertaining : Participant demographics Adverse effects Trial discontinuation Lab results Visit information Study site location /E criteria Study metadata Default, package constructs data create KRIs available {gsm} package.

what: Installation *Temporarily broken due to repo permissions issue*
title: Synthetic Test Data Generator
text: can install development version gsm.datasim GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("Gilead-BioStats/gsm.datasim@dev")

title: Synthetic Test Data Generator
text: See inst/examples/run_gsm_workflows.R script run gsm workflows using data generated raw_data_generator. Result analysis reporting data along html report outputs.
code: # Generate raw data using specified parameters  data <- raw_data_generator(ParticipantCount = 100, SiteCount = 10, StudyID = "Study01", SnapshotCount = 5)   # Generate raw data using specified parameters and Mappings  data <- raw_data_generator(ParticipantCount = 100,                             SiteCount = 10,                             StudyID = "Study01",                             SnapshotCount = 5,                             workflow_path = "workflow/1_mappings",                             mappings = c("Raw_STUDY", "Raw_SITE", "Raw_SUBJ", "Raw_ENROLL",)   # Generate raw data using a template file (currently it runs on a smaller template but not always on a larger one),  # this is our current goto sim process:  data <- raw_data_generator(template_path = "~/gsm.datasim/data-raw/small_template.csv")

title: Create Study Configuration for Standard Datasets — create_standard_study_config
text: study_id Study identifier participant_count Number participants site_count Number sites analytics_package Analytics package use analytics_workflows Specific workflows run study Include study metadata (Raw_STUDY) subjects Include subject demographics (Raw_SUBJ) sites_data Include site information (Raw_SITE) adverse_events Include adverse event data protocol_deviations Include protocol deviation data lab_data Include laboratory data subject_visits Include subject visit data (Raw_SV) visit_schedule Include visit schedule data (Raw_VISIT) enrollment Include enrollment data data_changes Include data change tracking (Raw_DATACHG) data_entry Include data entry tracking (Raw_DATAENT) queries Include query data (Raw_QUERY) pharmacokinetics Include pharmacokinetics data study_drug_completion Include study drug completion (Raw_SDRGCOMP) study_completion Include overall study completion (Raw_STUDCOMP) inclusion_exclusion Include inclusion/exclusion criteria (Raw_IE) exclusions Include exclusion tracking (Raw_EXCLUSION) country Include country mapping

what: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — .gsm_datasim_runtime_state
title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — .gsm_datasim_runtime_state
text: function generates raw data study snapshots based provided participant count, site count, study ID, snapshot count. parameters NULL, loads template CSV file iterate multiple study configurations. generated raw data saved RDS file.

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — .gsm_datasim_runtime_state
code: .gsm_datasim_runtime_state

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — .gsm_datasim_runtime_state
text: object class environment length 1.

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — .gsm_datasim_runtime_state
text: ParticipantCount integer specifying number participants study. NULL, function use values template file. SiteCount integer specifying number sites study. NULL, function use values template file. StudyID string specifying study identifier. NULL, function use values template file. SnapshotCount integer specifying number snapshots study. NULL, function use values template file. SnapshotWidth character specifying frequency snapshots, defaults "months". Accepts "days", "weeks", "months" "years". User can also place number unit "3 months". template_path string specifying path template CSV file. Default "~/gsm.datasim/inst/template.csv". workflow_path string specifying path workflow mappings. Default "workflow/1_mappings". generate_reports boolean, specifying whether produce reports upon execution. Default FALSE. mappings string specifying names workflows run. package string specifying package workflows used MakeWorkflowList() located. Default "gsm". strStartDate string denote first snapshot simulated data occurs save boolean, specifying whether saved RDS generation_mode Generation backend use: "core" (default) "legacy".

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — .gsm_datasim_runtime_state
text: list raw data generated study snapshot, saved RDS file "data-raw/raw_data.RDS".

title: Generate Raw Data for Study Snapshots — .gsm_datasim_runtime_state
text: function performs following steps: ParticipantCount, SiteCount, StudyID, SnapshotCount NULL, function reads template.csv file get necessary parameters multiple studies. generates raw data study snapshots based either provided parameters template file. generated data saved RDS file returned list.

title: Export Complete Study Data to Disk — export_study_data
text: study longitudinal_study object (output create_longitudinal_study quick_longitudinal_study). output_dir Root directory study folder created. Defaults current working directory. study_folder Optional name top-level study folder.  Defaults study$study_id characters invalid folder names replaced underscores. overwrite TRUE, existing CSV files silently overwritten.  FALSE (default), error raised target study folder already exists. save_rds TRUE, analytics_full.rds file also written per snapshot, preserving non-data.frame objects (workflow lists, summaries, etc.) expressed flat CSVs. Defaults FALSE. verbose TRUE, prints progress messages.  Defaults FALSE.

text: desired_specs list specifications data types included. SnapshotCount integer specifying number snapshots generate. SnapshotWidth character specifying frequency snapshots, defaults "months". Accepts "days", "weeks", "months" "years". User can also place number unit "3 months". ParticipantCount integer specifying number participants study. SiteCount integer specifying number sites study. StudyID string specifying study identifier. workflow_path string specifying path workflow mappings. mappings string specifying names workflows run. package string specifying package workflows used MakeWorkflowList() located. strStartDate string denote first snapshot simulated data occurs

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple study with standard datasets using config approach config <- create_study_config("ONCOLOGY001", participant_count = 200, site_count = 15) %>%   set_temporal_config(start_date = "2023-01-01", snapshot_count = 12, snapshot_width = "months") %>%   add_dataset_config("Raw_AE", enabled = TRUE,                      count_formula = function(config, snapshot_idx = 1) {                       base_count <- config$study_params$participant_count * 2.5                       factor <- snapshot_idx / config$temporal_config$snapshot_count                       round(base_count * factor)                     }) %>%   add_dataset_config("Raw_SV", enabled = TRUE) %>%   add_dataset_config("Raw_VISIT", enabled = TRUE) study_data <- generate_study_data(config)  # Using convenience function for standard datasets config <- create_standard_study_config("TRIAL002", participant_count = 100, site_count = 10,                                        adverse_events = TRUE, lab_data = TRUE)  # Custom dataset configuration config <- create_study_config("CUSTOM001", participant_count = 300, site_count = 20) %>%   add_dataset_config("Raw_Biomarker", enabled = TRUE,                     count_formula = function(config) config$study_params$participant_count * 5,                     dependencies = "Raw_SUBJ") study_data <- generate_study_data(config) } # }

title: gsm.datasim v1.1.2
text: release resolves bug involving duplicate records randomization domain.

title: gsm.datasim v1.1.1
text: release adds new contributor guidelines standardized issue templates.

title: gsm.datasim v1.1.0
text: New Mapping Workflows Support: - Additional workflow support “IE” domain - dates/timestamp support many domains now available.

title: gsm.datasim v1.0.0
text: excited announce first major release gsm.datasim package, collection functions generate synthetic test data RBQM Clinical Trials based several parameters.

title: gsm.datasim v1.0.0
text: User-facing functions - generate_rawdata_for_single_study() can create snapshot(s) single study provided proper parameters appropriate mapping specifications. - raw_data_generator() wrapper run generate_rawdata_for_single_study() create multiple studies providing template/dataset containing variety parameters. New Mapping Workflows Support: - workflows exist gsm.mapping’s inst/workflow/1_mappings now supported, “STUDY”, “SUBJ”, “SITE” “ENROLL” always required. - many gsm.endpoints specific domains, Anticancer OverallResponse now support well. Replacing clindata gsm.datasim: - object gsm.core::lSource created using gsm.datasim examples, tests, vignettes across gsm ecosystem. object based “core mappings” include: “AE”, “COUNTRY”, “DATACHG”, “DATAENT”, “ENROLL”, “LB”, “PD”, “PK”, “QUERY”, “STUDY”, “STUDCOMP”, “SDRGCOMP”, “SITE”, “SUBJ”
